description: A heap-buffer-overflow (out-of-bounds write) vulnerability exists in V8's JSON serializer (JsonStringifier::SerializeString_). When JSON.stringify serializes an array containing strings, the serializer allocates an output buffer (two_byte_ptr_) via ChangeEncoding() upon encountering a two-byte string. Subsequently, when serializing a one-byte string element via SerializeString_<uint8_t, uint16_t, false>, the slow path's AppendSubstringByCopy function can write past the end of the allocated output buffer. The vulnerability is triggered by using V8's sandbox memory corruption API (v8_enable_memory_corruption_api=true, --sandbox-fuzzing) to change the length field of a string value embedded in the backing FixedArray during serialization, creating an inconsistency between the output buffer's allocated size and the amount of data written. This results in a write at two_byte_ptr_[part_length_] (immediately past the end of the buffer allocated by ChangeEncoding()), achieving a sandbox escape via out-of-bounds write.

fixes.description: Patch generated from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/6172045 to avoid remote fetch during validation.
fixes.evidence_text: f62b0d60d5bd4a13e1ac077bf51d71941dad75b6 [json] Stringify: Avoid int overflows with negative string length
